pr.title: Integration PR #145 — M4/M2/M3 Validation Packets v1
pr.description:
## Summary

Planning deliverable (no framework, no implementation). Converts PR #144's **negative discovery** into three concise, implementation-ready **validation packets** so the next executable PR (#146+) can begin without redoing discovery. Adds `docs/architecture/integration/` (index + 3 packets); touches no source.

PR #144 answered *"what is disconnected/unwired?"*; PR #145 answers *"how do we validate the implementation that fixes the highest-value disconnected behavior?"*

**Evidence grounding** — PR #144's gap analyzer re-run against current `main` (which now includes PR #142 `execution_report.py`, `dc99c70`): 158 modules / 275 edges / 9 isolated / 16 gaps. All **5 execution-chain transitions** flagged `declared_but_unwired_chains` — *"no import edge in either direction"* → the chain is **nominal, not executable**. EVID-001 (`model_health`) → M2; PURP-001 (all 158 modules) → M3.

### Packets

| Packet | Milestone (INV) | Next PR (proposed) | Role |
|---|---|---|---|
| M4 — Execution Chain Validation | INV-03 | Integration PR #146 — Execution Chain Orchestrator v1 | **next executable target** |
| M2 — Evidence→Promotion | INV-04 | Evidence-to-State Loop v1 | hooks + test matrix |
| M3 — Purpose & Layer | INV-05 | Purpose & Layer Index v1 | hooks + test matrix |

### M4 highlights (the prize)

- Preserves *"docstring read-only consumption is NOT sufficient"* — defines pass/fail as **real id equality**: `downstream.ref(reference_type==UpstreamType).reference_value == upstream.<id>`, plus a gap-analysis diff showing `declared_but_unwired_chains` drop 5→0 as wiring lands.
- Wiring, not new objects: each stage already has a `*Reference`/`*ReferenceType` hook naming its upstream (Step→`PLAN`, Attempt→`STEP`, Result→`ATTEMPT`, Artifact→`RESULT`, Report→`RESULT`/`ARTIFACT`). `ExecutionReport` confirmed on `main` → terminal stage (PR #144's "module not present" flag was a branch-timing artifact, resolved).
- Candidate decision table compares an **internal deterministic capability (recommended slice subject)** vs **`SetParameterValue`** (deferred — hard Revit-runtime dep, not CI-deterministic) vs **`InventoryModel`** (rejected — broad + guarded), justified from repo evidence.

### Discipline / bounds honored

- M4 stays the next executable target; M2/M3 are hooks + matrices, not reasons to delay M4.
- M2 defines tests on existing `global_capability_registry` / `capability_confidence` (no promotion doctrine). M3 keeps **structural vs semantic vs executable** relationships distinct (no new semantic framework).
- Re-architecture (Execution Graph Synthesizer, Org State schema, promotion doctrine, semantic schema) routed to INV-01/02/05/07 and Programs 0/2/6/7 as unresolved — **not designed here**.
- Canonical docs cross-referenced by **stable filenames**; uploaded version-suffixed copies + the fact that canonical docs are not yet committed are flagged as source-structure observations. **No canonical filename migration** in this PR (reference rule only).

## What did not change

No source code, no tests, no behavior. Docs-only. 2024 Revit baseline unaffected; no live Revit path touched.


Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
pr.author: plamen-dev

commits.author: plamen.hristov@imsdevlab.onmicrosoft.com
commits.message:
Integration PR #145 — M4/M2/M3 Validation Packets v1

Convert PR #144 gap-analysis findings into narrow, implementation-ready
validation targets for the next executable PRs. Planning deliverable only
(no framework, no implementation).

- M4 (next executable target): execution-chain id-flow validation, candidate
  decision table, exact pass/fail, re-arch routed to INV-01/Program 2.
- M2 (hooks + matrix): evidence->state/confidence/promotion using existing
  global_capability_registry / capability_confidence; refs EVID-001.
- M3 (hooks + matrix): purpose/layer via capability_summary; structural vs
  semantic vs executable kept distinct; refs PURP-001.
- Index + canonical source-status note (stable filenames; no migration).

Co-Authored-By: Devin AI <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
